pages.raw_text:
The Spokane County Referendum
league will have introduced into
the city council this evening an
ordinance which, II passed, will
give the voters of the city the priv
ilege of voting on all public utility
franchises. As the recent demo-
cratic convention went on record
as being in favor of the initiative
and referendum and the present
city administration Is democratic,
referendum advocates think there
is every reason to believe the or
dinance will pass. Councilmen
Pratt and Laumer are in favor Of
it. both being members of the local
referendum league.


The proposed ordinance provides
that public utility franchises shall
be submitted to vote on petition Of
10 per cent Of registered voters and
granted or rejected as dictated by
the majority voting.


Instructions to public officials
are also provided for on petition Of
Is per cent Of voters and prece-
dence over all other business Is
asked for direct initiative petition.
